text.title: cloud-run-traffic-management Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for cloud-run-traffic-management as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Deploying directly to production with 100% traffic without a staging or tagged-revision test step.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: A bad deployment immediately affects all users. The time to detect and rollback is measured in minutes (monitoring alert + human response), during which errors accumulate. Tagged deployments reduce impact to zero until validated.

failure-mode.description: Using --to-latest in canary rollout instead of --to-tags=canary=N.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: --to-latest routes 100% traffic to the newest revision immediately, bypassing canary validation. Always use --to-tags with explicit percentages during staged rollouts.
